reasoning_model_1: Model 1 accurately synthesizes the content with perfect adherence to the script 'This weekend, should we go to the movies or the park?', without any additions or omissions. In terms of the required Cognitive State (hesitant tone), the model performs exceptionally well. There are distinct, thoughtful pauses inserted at key moments: 'This weekend... [0:01] should we go to the movies... [0:02] or... [0:03] the park?'. The pacing is deliberately slow, and the intonation rises uncertainly at the end of phrases, perfectly simulating a person weighing options in real-time. The prosody sounds natural and human-like.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 fails significantly in Content Accuracy. While it includes the target sentence, it hallucinates and adds a substantial amount of conversational filler that was not requested ('I mean, I'm not sure. Either way sounds fine...'). Regarding the Cognitive State, the model fails to apply the 'hesitant tone' to the specific target sentence. The target sentence 'This weekend, should we go to the movies or the park?' is spoken rapidly and fluidly (0:00-0:02) without any sign of hesitation. The hesitation only appears in the hallucinated text that follows. Therefore, it fails to execute the command on the requested text.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It followed the text instructions perfectly and applied the requested paralinguistic feature (hesitation) directly to the target sentence through effective use of pauses and pacing. Model 2 failed on two counts: it hallucinated extra text, and it failed to apply the hesitant tone to the actual target sentence, instead rushing through the target sentence and only acting hesitant in the unauthorized added content.
